steps.name: Given  Close tutorial screen
Then  Welcome screen is opened
When  Close welcome screen
Then  Add library screen is opened
When  Add library "LYRASIS Reads" on Add library screen
Then  Account "LYRASIS Reads" is present on Accounts screen
When  Enter credentials for 'LYRASIS Reads' account
Then  Login is performed successfully
When  Open Catalog
And  Open search modal
When  Search 'available' book of distributor 'Palace Marketplace' and bookType 'EBOOK' from "LYRASIS Reads" and save as 'bookNameInfo'
And  Click GET action button on EBOOK book with 'bookNameInfo' bookName on catalog books screen and save book as 'bookInfo'
And  Open EBOOK book with READ action button and 'bookNameInfo' bookName on catalog books screen and save book as 'bookInfo'
And  Click READ action button on book details screen
And  Scroll page forward from 7 to 10 times
And  Save pageNumber as 'pageNumberKey' and chapterName as 'chapterNameKey' on epub reader screen
And  Wait for 3 seconds
And  Return to previous screen for epub and pdf
And  Click READ action button on book details screen
Then  'bookInfo' book is present on epub reader screen
And  PageNumber 'pageNumberKey' is correct
And  Scroll page forward from 3 to 4 times
And  Save pageNumber as 'pageNumberKey' and chapterName as 'chapterNameKey' on epub reader screen
And  Wait for 3 seconds
And  Restart app
And  Open Books
Then  EBOOK book with READ action button and 'bookInfo' bookInfo is present on books screen

Then  'bookInfo' book is present on epub reader screen

Book is not present on epub reader screen. Expected bookName - 'The Tugboater Family', actualName - 'Lonnie worked alone on the small tug used to move the dump scows at the dredge site and hardly ventured beyond the dredging area, but he got on the radio and told Captain Grimes to handle his bridge passage with his loaded scow, and Lonnie would handle the rescue. The second man, whom Lonnie had seen fall overboard, had now retrieved the life ring and was swimming toward the struggling man. Lonnie left his empty scow with the deckhands on the dredge, and he headed toward the two men in the water. His safety training kicked in, as he circled wide around the men, not wanting them to be harmed by the tug’s propeller. Lonnie positioned his tug downstream of the two men, who were now together, and stopped his propeller. Then he went out on deck and grabbed one of his tug’s life rings and tied the lanyard to the handrail, throwing the ring toward the men. It landed just short of them, but the man with the lifejacket was able to drag the other man toward the second life ring. Both men were exhausted and hanging onto the life rings for dear life. Lonnie was not a big man, just 5’-9”, but the adrenaline from this situation had kicked in. Once they were both hanging on, Lonnie pulled them to the side of his tug and was able to drag them over the side of the tug’s low bulwark.'
